name: regextokens
description: Modernized regex patterns for OAuth / API token scanning.
flavor: RE2-compatible (Go) and Python re. No lookbehind/backreferences.

strategy_legend.prefix: Distinctive fixed prefix; regex reliable on its own.
strategy_legend.keyword: Low entropy / no prefix; gate on a nearby keyword.

samples_note: Positive/negative test tokens are defined in build_patterns.py and exercised by tests/test_patterns.py. They are intentionally omitted here: a sample that matches a pattern also matches issuer secret scanners, which would block commits.

patterns.name: Base64 string
patterns.notes: Format validator, not a secret. Standard Base64 alphabet with padding. Anchored.

patterns.name: JSON Web Token (JWT)
patterns.notes: Three base64url segments. Header begins ey (i.e. {"). Decode to inspect; signature alone is not the secret.

patterns.name: PEM private key block
patterns.notes: Highest-value single indicator. Matches the header line of any PEM-encoded private key.

patterns.name: Access key ID
patterns.notes: 20 chars. AKIA = long-term IAM key. ASIA = temporary STS/session key. Body is base32 (A-Z, 2-7).

patterns.name: Secret access key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: 40-char base64 body has no fixed prefix and is low entropy. Gate on an 'aws' keyword or it matches almost any 40-char token.

patterns.name: API key
patterns.notes: 39 chars total. Covers Maps, Firebase, and most GCP API keys.

patterns.name: OAuth 2.0 client ID
patterns.notes: Client ID is public, but pins config to a project. Ends in .apps.googleusercontent.com.

patterns.name: OAuth 2.0 client secret
patterns.notes: Current client-secret format. Replaced the old unprefixed 24-char secret.

patterns.name: OAuth 2.0 access token
patterns.notes: Short-lived. Prefix ya29. then variable-length body.

patterns.name: OAuth 2.0 refresh token
patterns.notes: Begins 1// (note double slash). Replaces the older single-slash 1/ format.

patterns.name: Personal access token (classic)
patterns.notes: All GitHub v2 tokens carry a checksum in the body; shape match is necessary, not sufficient.

patterns.name: Personal access token (fine-grained)
patterns.notes: 82 chars after the prefix (22 + '_' + 59).

patterns.name: Personal access token
patterns.notes: GitLab moved all tokens to typed gl* prefixes.

patterns.name: API key
patterns.notes: Modern keys embed the literal T3BlbkFJ (base64 'OpenAI') mid-token. Optional sub-prefixes proj-/svcacct-/admin-.

patterns.name: API key (legacy, no longer issued)
patterns.notes: Pre-2024 format. High false-positive rate (any sk- + 48 alnum). Prefer the T3BlbkFJ-anchored rule above.

patterns.name: API key
patterns.notes: Body is fixed length and ends in AA.

patterns.name: Access token
patterns.notes: Prefix introduced 2021; final chars encode a CRC32 checksum.

patterns.name: Upload token
patterns.notes: Macaroon. The static segment AgEIcHlwaS5vcmc is base64 'pypi.org'.

patterns.name: Secret / restricted key
patterns.notes: sk_ = full secret, rk_ = restricted (scoped). live/test mode. Length now variable (legacy 24, newer keys far longer).

patterns.name: Publishable key
patterns.notes: Client-side, not secret, but useful for environment fingerprinting.

patterns.name: Production access token
patterns.notes: Current OAuth/personal access token format.

patterns.name: Access token (legacy)
patterns.notes: Prefix is sq-zero-atp. The capital-O 'sqOatp' in older lists is wrong.

patterns.name: OAuth secret (legacy)
patterns.notes: Prefix is sq-zero-csp.

patterns.name: Access token
patterns.notes: Dollar-delimited: access_token$production$<16>$<32>. The mangled entry in older lists never compiled.

patterns.name: API key (DEPRECATED service)
patterns.notes: Picatic shut down in 2018. Kept for historical scanning only. Note the prefix collides with Stripe-style sk_live_.

patterns.name: Admin API access token
patterns.notes: shpat_ = admin API. Related prefixes: shpss_ (shared secret), shppa_ (private app), shpca_ (custom app).

patterns.name: Bot token (xoxb)
patterns.notes: Segment lengths are now variable; fixed {11}-{11}-{24} from older lists misses current tokens.

patterns.name: Incoming webhook URL
patterns.notes: Full URL is the secret. Old bare T../B../.. shape produced false positives.

patterns.name: Account SID
patterns.notes: Prefix AC + 32 hex. Older '55...' lists were incorrect.

patterns.name: API key SID
patterns.notes: Pairs with a 32-hex secret (no prefix; gate on a 'twilio' keyword).

patterns.name: API key
patterns.notes: Trailing -us<dc> is the datacenter. The 32-hex prefix alone is not distinctive.

patterns.name: Bot token
patterns.notes: Three dot-separated base64url segments; first encodes the bot user ID. Approximate; verify by decoding segment 1.

patterns.name: App-only bearer token
patterns.notes: Begins with a long run of 'A' (base64 padding), then URL-encoded body.

patterns.name: OAuth 1.0a access token
patterns.notes: <numeric user id>-<40 alnum>. Structural; pair with a 'twitter' keyword to cut noise.

patterns.name: Username / handle
patterns.notes: Public handle, not a secret. 1-15 word chars, not preceded by @ or word char.

patterns.name: Access token
patterns.notes: Graph API tokens begin EAA. The old fixed 'EAACEdEose0cBA' app prefix is obsolete.

patterns.name: Access token
patterns.notes: Instagram Basic Display was deprecated Dec 2024; current long-lived tokens (IGQ / Facebook EAA) are issued via the Instagram API with Facebook Login.

patterns.name: Username
patterns.notes: Public handle. RE2-safe rewrite of the old lookahead-based pattern.

patterns.name: Service API key
patterns.notes: Current Places API key prefix fsq3. The old '[0-9a-zA-Z_]{5,31}' client key was too generic to detect; the original list also mis-typed it as a character class.

patterns.name: Personal token
patterns.notes: Other Doppler scopes use dp.st. (service), dp.sa. (service account).

patterns.name: Personal access token
patterns.notes: OAuth token dor_v1_ / refresh dor_v1_ share the same shape.

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated UUID)
patterns.notes: Legacy keys are bare UUIDs - indistinguishable from any UUID without a 'heroku' keyword. Newer keys use the HRKU- prefix.

patterns.name: Auth token (DEPRECATED API)
patterns.notes: amzn.mws.<UUID>. MWS retired in favor of SP-API (Login with Amazon tokens, prefixes Atza|/Atzr|). Original list entry was malformed.

patterns.name: API token (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: 40-char token, no fixed prefix; gate on a 'cloudflare' keyword or it matches any 40-char string.

patterns.name: Global API key (legacy, keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: Legacy 37-hex global key; keyword-gated. Prefer scoped API tokens.

patterns.name: Origin CA key
patterns.notes: Distinctive v1.0- prefix; regex-reliable.

patterns.name: Access token (2024+ format)
patterns.notes: Prefixed format introduced 2024. Related: vck_ (AI Gateway), cl_ (OAuth client id).

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: 32-hex key, no prefix; gate on a 'datadog' keyword. Often paired with a 40-hex application key.

patterns.name: Application key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: 40-hex application key, no prefix; keyword-gated.

patterns.name: API key
patterns.notes: waka_ + UUID.

patterns.name: Tokenization key (publishable)
patterns.notes: Environment-prefixed and publishable (client-side, not a secret) - like Stripe pk_.

patterns.name: Storage account access key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: 88-char base64 (86 chars + '=='), a 512-bit key. Same shape as Cosmos DB / Service Bus / Event Hubs SharedAccessKey; the keyword gate disambiguates. 'AccountKey' and 'SharedAccessKey' are Microsoft's own detection keywords.

patterns.name: Azure DevOps personal access token (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: New PATs are 84 chars (52 randomized); legacy PATs are 52 chars. No reliable standalone prefix, so gated on an Azure DevOps keyword. The '84 chars / 52 random' length is documented (sprint 241); the exact body charset and any embedded marker are NOT primary-documented, so this stays keyword-gated rather than a fixed-offset structural rule. Microsoft is deprecating PATs in favor of Entra tokens.

patterns.name: Service token (hvs.)
patterns.notes: Vault 1.10+ typed prefix: hvs.=service, hvb.=batch, hvr.=recovery (legacy s./b./r.). Vault documents tokens as opaque with '24 or more' random chars; length left open. Server-side-consistent and namespaced tokens run much longer (base64url body, hence _-).

patterns.name: Batch token (hvb.)
patterns.notes: Batch tokens are long encrypted blobs; the hvb. prefix is the reliable anchor.

patterns.name: HCP Terraform / Terraform Cloud API token
patterns.notes: Distinctive '.atlasv1.' infix (Atlas-era marker). One shape covers user, team, organization, audit, and agent tokens, and Terraform Enterprise; type is not distinguishable from shape.

patterns.name: Docker Hub personal access token
patterns.notes: Prefix dckr_pat_ is documented; body length is community-observed (~27), hence the range. Sibling dckr_oat_ = organization access token. Older Hub tokens were bare UUIDs.

patterns.name: Personal API token (CCIPAT)
patterns.notes: CCIPAT_<base58 id>_<40-hex>, introduced July 2023 (CCIPRJ_ for project tokens). Base58 alphabet excludes 0/O/I/l. Legacy tokens are bare 40-hex (only catchable keyword-gated).

patterns.name: Project API token (CCIPRJ)
patterns.notes: Project-scoped sibling of CCIPAT_.

patterns.name: Service account token (glsa_)
patterns.notes: glsa_<32 alnum>_<8-hex checksum>. Grafana added the trailing checksum specifically for scanner validation. Replaces legacy base64 API keys (eyJrIjoi...).

patterns.name: Cloud access policy token (glc_)
patterns.notes: glc_ + base64 of a JSON payload, so the body always begins 'eyJ'. Length varies with the embedded token name. May carry base64 '=' padding (no trailing \b).

patterns.name: User auth token (sntryu_)
patterns.notes: sntryu_ + 64-hex (token_hex(32)). First-party prefixes: sntryu_=user, sntrys_=org, sntrya_=user-app, sntryi_=integration. Legacy tokens are bare 64-hex.

patterns.name: Organization auth token (sntrys_)
patterns.notes: sntrys_ + base64(JSON payload, begins 'eyJ') + '_' + 43-char base64 secret (token_bytes(32), padding stripped). Exactly two underscores overall. Secret may end in +// (no trailing \b).

patterns.name: Auth / API / OAuth key (tskey-)
patterns.notes: tskey-<type>-<keyID>-<secret>; types api/auth/client/scim/webhook. Keys are case-sensitive. Segment lengths are not formally documented (ranged). Auth keys are the most commonly leaked (headless/CI use).

patterns.name: Access token (pul-)
patterns.notes: Prefix pul- is documented; the 40-hex body is from Pulumi's earlier published example (current docs show only the prefix). Personal, org, and team tokens all share pul-.

patterns.name: Personal access token (nfp_)
patterns.notes: Nov 2023 format: nf-family prefixes nfp_ (PAT), nfc_ (CLI), nfo_ (OAuth), nfu_ (app), nfb_ (build); 40-char envelope. Pre-Nov-2023 tokens are unprefixed.

patterns.name: API token / macaroon (fm2_)
patterns.notes: Macaroon: MsgPack-encoded, base64'd, then 'fm2_' prepended (per Fly's own repo, 'so it's easy to grep for them'). Sent as 'FlyV1 fm2_...'; multiples are comma-joined, each with its own fm2_.

patterns.name: Webhook signing secret (whsec_)
patterns.notes: HMAC signing secret (verify-only, but still sensitive: it lets an attacker forge webhook events). Prefix documented; body length empirical (32 dashboard, up to 64 from `stripe listen`).

patterns.name: API key (gsk_)
patterns.notes: Prefix gsk_ documented in Groq's own examples; body length community-observed (~52), hence the range.

patterns.name: API token (r8_)
patterns.notes: Prefix r8_; body length (~37) inferred from a length-preserving mask in Replicate's docs (r8_Hw + 35 masked chars), hence the range.

patterns.name: API key (sk-or-v1-)
patterns.notes: Distinctive sk-or-v1- prefix distinguishes from OpenAI sk-; match this before any generic sk- rule. Body observed as 64-hex; ranged to avoid false precision.

patterns.name: API key (xai-)
patterns.notes: Prefix xai- is solid; docs example is ~84 chars (~80 body). Exact length not formally documented, hence the range.

patterns.name: API key (pplx-)
patterns.notes: Prefix pplx- also appears in Perplexity model/product names (pplx-api, pplx-70b-*); the 32-char floor prevents matching those. Body length empirical.

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: DeepSeek keys are sk-prefixed and collide fully with OpenAI (and partially OpenRouter) sk-. Keyword gate is mandatory, mirroring the AWS secret-key precedent. Body length empirical.

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: Mistral documents no prefix, length, or format (keys are opaque). The 32-alnum body is observed-only; keyword-gated to stay usable.

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: Cohere documents no key format (only 'BEARER [API_KEY]'). The 40-alnum body is observed-only; keyword-gated.

patterns.name: API key (keyword-gated fw_)
patterns.notes: Prefix fw_ is documented but short and collision-prone (fw_version...); keyword-gated rather than shipped as a bare prefix. Body length empirical (docs example is truncated).

patterns.name: Organization API token (legacy, api_org_)
patterns.notes: Legacy org token (deprecated and blocked in the huggingface_hub client). Current tokens use hf_. Kept for historical scanning of old leaks. Body length undocumented.

patterns.name: App-level token (xapp)
patterns.notes: Prefix xapp- is documented (app-level tokens, Socket Mode). Segment structure (xapp-<n>-A<appid>-<digits>-<hex>) is inferred from Slack's synthetic examples plus field observation.

patterns.name: Bot token
patterns.notes: <bot_id>:AA<body>; body starts 'AA' (base64url). Length from the docs example ('a string, like ...'), not a hard spec. Leading \b won't match when the token is glued to the word 'bot' in api.telegram.org URLs.

patterns.name: Service account key file marker
patterns.notes: Fingerprints a GCP service-account key file. The live secret is the 'private_key' value in the same file (caught by private-key-block). Documented JSON shape includes type/private_key_id/private_key/client_email.

patterns.name: age X25519 secret key
patterns.notes: Bech32 with HRP 'AGE-SECRET-KEY-'; a 32-byte key encodes to 58 chars after the '1' separator (uppercase). Recipients (age1...) are public, not secrets. Post-quantum variant uses HRP AGE-SECRET-KEY-PQ-.

patterns.name: Cloud API token (ATATT3)
patterns.notes: Atlassian staff document the 'ATAT' token-prefix family (ATAT=API token, ATBB=app password, ATCT=access token); the full 'ATATT3' anchor is empirically constant. Atlassian states tokens are variable-length ('do not rely on fixed API token length'), so only the prefix is anchored. Pre-Dec-2024 tokens expired by May 2026.

patterns.name: Integration token (ntn_)
patterns.notes: ntn_ replaced the secret_ prefix for new tokens (Sept 2024), explicitly to help secret scanners. Notion advises against regex-validating tokens (treat as opaque), so body length is ranged. Legacy secret_ tokens still work.

patterns.name: Personal access token
patterns.notes: pat<14-char id>.<64-hex secret>. Airtable documents only that PATs are 'prefixed with their ID' and are otherwise opaque/variable — the '.'+64-hex body is empirical (scanner consensus). Legacy 'key'+14 API keys were deprecated Feb 2024.

patterns.name: API key (PMAK-)
patterns.notes: PMAK-<24-hex>-<34-hex>. Prefix appears in Postman's own docs placeholders; the two-segment hex split is scanner consensus. Collection access keys use PMAT-.

patterns.name: API key (rubygems_)
patterns.notes: rubygems_ + 48-hex, proven by the docs' own ~/.gem/credentials example. Pre-2021 legacy keys were bare 32-hex (only catchable via the :rubygems_api_key: YAML context).

patterns.name: Short-lived access token (sl.)
patterns.notes: Short-lived (4h) OAuth tokens carry the 'sl.' prefix (Dropbox staff-confirmed); long-lived tokens are unprefixed and now deprecated. The prefix is only 3 chars, so the 130-char length floor does the disambiguation. Newer sl.u. variant adds a '.'.

patterns.name: API token (SSWS, keyword-gated)
patterns.notes: Okta's proprietary SSWS tokens start '00' and are ~42 chars; the middle is elided in docs, so length/charset are partly empirical. Gated on the 'SSWS' scheme keyword (or 'okta'). Tokens expire after 30 idle days.
